headline: Micron joins $1 trillion club as AI race powers memory chip boom
summary: STORY: :: MicronMicron Technology topped $1 trillion in market value for the first time on Tuesday...crowning a dizzying rally that has cemented the largest U.S. memory chipmaker as one of the standout winners of the AI boom.Micron's shares rose more than 18% on Tuesday, pushing the stock to a record high after brokerage firm UBS more than tripled its price target on the stock to over $1,600.The milestone underscores the central role of memory chips - used to store and move data - in AI infrastructure...and reflects a broadening of the AI trade beyond chipmakers like Nvidia whose processors train and run AI models.Micron's ascent also gives the U.S. a strong contender in a memory-chip race that has largely been led by Asia so far.South Korea's Samsung Electronics, the world's top memory chipmaker, has already hit the $1 trillion milestone, while the country's SK Hynix is also closing in.Chip stocks in general continued their surge on Tuesday, with Marvell Technology rising more than 10% in morning trading, while Qualcomm added as much as 8%. Shares of Broadcom and Intel were also higher.All helped push the Philadelphia Semiconductor index up nearly 6% to an all-time high.

headline: Bond Markets Are Reluctant Participants as Peace Hopes Fuel Stocks and Oil Prices
summary: U.S. stocks looked set to test all-time highs again Tuesday, with the tech-led  leading early gainers, as investors bet the war in Iran was nearing an interim peace agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz and unleash energy markets ahead of the summer driving demand expected in domestic markets.  Bond markets, however, remained reluctant to fully commit to the new peace talks, and yields were holding north of key levels ahead of a key set of growth and inflation figures later in the week.  Talks between Washington and Tehran, however, have been punctuated by a fresh round of attacks by U.S. troops on targets in southern Iran as well as claims from Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on Tuesday that it fired on a U.S. fighter jet that entered the country’s airspace.

headline: Tech Weekly: Ferrari's electric bet, Samsung's major deal
summary: STORY: From Ferrari's electric bet to Samsung's major union deal, this is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyFerrari presented its first fully electric car.The Luce has a range of over 300 miles and features four electric motors which help it reach a top speed of around 200mph.It marks a high-stakes shift as the sports car maker gambles new consumers will shift to high-tech luxury EVs.The Luce is aimed at families with deep pockets with a price tag of $640,000.Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang signaled he sees major long-term opportunities for the firm in China.That's despite tensions over a tech race between Washington and Beijing.Huang spoke to reporters in Taipei, ahead of a trade show.He was asked whether China was part of a $200-billion market forecast he mentioned during a recent Nvidia earnings call."I would think so. $200 billion is not today, it’s someday. And it includes all the data centers, CPUs. This is a new market for us."Lenovo reported a better-than-expected 27% jump in quarterly revenueThe tech firm's tablet and smartphone division saw revenue rise by close to a quarter - it's highest growth rate in five years.Lenovo said overall revenue for the period to the end of March jumped $21.6 billion - above analyst estimates.Investors liked what they heard and shares soared 15% soon after the update.Texas sued WhatsApp and its parent firm Meta.The state said the tech firm misled consumers over the messaging app’s encryption and privacy standards.The lawsuit says users are given false assurance that their messages are encrypted.A Meta spokesman called the allegations “false”, and said WhatsApp can't access people’s encrypted messages.Samsung shares surged after it reached a deal with its South Korean union to avoid an 18-day strike.48,000 workers could have taken part in the proposed industrial action.One union source said a memory chip worker with a base salary of about $53,000 is expected to receive a bonus of around $416,000.There was relief in Seoul at the deal as Samsung makes up for about a quarter of the country's exports.Although the Samsung Electronics Co Union, which has about 13,000 members, took legal action to stop the vote on a pay deal - which is expected to be concluded by Wednesday.It said it took legal action after being told it had no right to join the vote.
